description: Dead links shipped to production repeatedly — doc links in hq-state.json not verified against actual files before deploy
prevention: Added pre-deploy validation script: python3 checks all doc links resolve to real .md files. Run before every git push.

description: UTC timestamps displayed instead of Mountain Time — all user-facing timestamps must be MT
prevention: All hq-state.json timestamps now use -06:00 offset. JS already uses timeZone: America/Denver. Nel audit should flag any Z-suffix timestamps in hq-state.json.

description: Findings reported without resolution status — Nel reports issues but doesn't track if they're fixed/pending/resolved
prevention: Nel view now shows status badges per finding category. Nel report should include resolution state going forward.

description: Task marked complete without verification — changes made to local files but not deployed or tested against live site
prevention: Post-completion checklist: 1) verify local file has changes, 2) git push succeeds, 3) Vercel deploy READY, 4) fetch live page and confirm content. Never mark done until step 4 passes.

description: Copy/paste commands given to Hyo instead of using queue — violates zero-copy-paste rule. Hyo time is the bottleneck.
prevention: CLAUDE.md hydration requires queue ping at session start. AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md ZERO COPY-PASTE RULE. Daily audit checks for [NEEDS HYO] items that should be queue commands. If queue is down, fix queue — never fall back to copy-paste.

description: Queue worker blocks on long commands — sleep 1800 inside a queue command blocked ALL work for 30 min because worker is single-threaded
prevention: Worker timeout capped at 300s (5 min max). healthcheck.sh no longer submits sleep-based recheck commands.

description: Python true/false vs True/False — bash variables like playbook_updated=false get substituted into Python inline code but Python uses True/False not true/false
prevention: All 5 agent runners fixed. Pattern: when embedding bash vars in python3 -c, use quoted strings 'False'/'True' not bare false/true.

description: grep -P not available on macOS BSD grep — causes silent failures in scripts running on Mini
prevention: Never use grep -P. Use grep -E for extended regex or grep -o for extraction. All scripts audited.

description: Research files were only 12-18 lines (briefs) causing Hyo to report links aren't accessible 4-5 times — the links worked but content was too thin to be useful
prevention: All entity/topic files enriched to 61-89 lines. New research must be substantive (>50 lines). Nel can flag thin files.

description: Cowork sandbox HYO_ROOT defaults to ~/Documents/Projects/Hyo which resolves to sandbox home, not mounted path — queue submissions silently go to wrong directory
prevention: Always set HYO_ROOT=/sessions/sharp-gracious-franklin/mnt/Hyo before calling submit.py from Cowork.

description: P0 overnight tasks (Aether migration, Hyo agent build) need a Kai session — launchd daemons only run shell scripts, not CEO-level work
prevention: Always audit: which tasks have triggers vs which need a session. Document in KAI_BRIEF trigger audit table. Don't promise overnight delivery for session-dependent work.

description: Monthly milestone targets create laxity — Hyo wants aggressive shipping
prevention: Milestones use 'ship ASAP / this week / start immediately' language. No monthly comfort zones. If it can ship today, it ships today.

description: ra.sh and sam.sh used 'stat -f %m' (macOS syntax) before 'stat -c %Y' (Linux) without OS detection — on Linux, stat -f outputs multi-line stdout before exiting non-zero, polluting PLAYBOOK_MTIME variable and causing 'File: unbound variable' in arithmetic expansion under set -u
fix: Swapped stat order: stat -c %Y first (Linux), stat -f %m second (macOS fallback) in ra.sh:419 and sam.sh:487
prevention: Always put Linux stat syntax first when writing cross-platform stat calls without uname detection. Pattern: stat -c %Y first, stat -f %m second.

description: aether-metrics.json was rewritten with currentPeriod instead of currentWeek during migration. hq.html and aether.sh both expected currentWeek. Dashboard showed $1000 default instead of real $90.25. FIX: rewrote JSON to use currentWeek with real data AND made hq.html/aether.sh resilient to both structures. PREVENTION: any time a data file schema changes, grep all consumers (hq.html, aether.sh, push_to_hq, self-evolution) and update them. Never change schema without updating consumers.
fix: rewrote aether-metrics.json, added fallback logic to hq.html and aether.sh
lesson: Hyo asked about this 4+ times. Schema mismatches are silent failures. Always test the rendered output, not just the data file.
resolution: Verified 2026-04-21: aether-metrics.json uses currentWeek (correct). hq.html reads data.currentWeek. No currentPeriod field present. Already fixed in prior session.

description: agents/nel/security/openai.key contains placeholder 'sk-your-***-here' instead of a real key. GPT daily log review and fact-checking will fail (HTTP 401) until Hyo places the real OpenAI API key. The old key was at ~/Documents/Projects/Kai/.env — check if that file still exists on the Mini.
fix: Hyo must place real OpenAI API key at agents/nel/security/openai.key
lesson: Always verify secrets exist with a real value, not just that the file exists.
resolution: Verified 2026-04-21: agents/nel/security/openai.key contains real API key (sk-proj-3QwLj...), not placeholder.

description: Data files existed and scripts exited 0, but HQ never rendered the output. Morning report JSON was generated but hq.html had no loadMorningReport() function. Aether metrics existed but dashboard showed defaults because of schema mismatch. Nel QA checked file existence but not rendered output. Simulation checked script exit codes but not HTML binding. ROOT CAUSE: Testing infrastructure (files exist, APIs respond) without testing user-facing output (data actually renders). FIX: Nel Phase 7.5 (Rendered Output Verification) checks data-to-HTML binding, morning report currency, Aether real data. Simulation Phase 6 checks rendered output pathways. PREVENTION: Every data file in website/data/ must have a corresponding render reference in hq.html. Nel verifies this q6h. Simulation verifies on every run.
lesson: Hyo caught this twice. Never test just the pipeline — test the rendered result. If data exists but nothing renders it, the data is useless.
resolution: Verified 2026-04-21: 8 data JSON files in agents/sam/website/data/, morning-report.json populated and rendering in HQ. Feed entry present.

description: Continuation session (context compaction) skipped hydration protocol entirely. Kai picked up from the summary and went straight to pending work without reading KAI_BRIEF, KAI_TASKS, known-issues, or simulation outcomes. System state had drifted (6 consecutive unhealthy healthchecks, new flags, daemon activity) but Kai operated on stale assumptions from the summary. ROOT CAUSE: CLAUDE.md said 'do this before responding to anything' but didn't explicitly cover continuation sessions. The continuation prompt said 'continue without asking questions' which overrode the hydration instruction. FIX: CLAUDE.md now has explicit bold-text rule: continuation sessions are NOT exempt from hydration. Summary provides task context but doesn't replace reading live system state. PREVENTION: Every new session — fresh or continued — reads hydration files first. No exceptions. Hyo's question 'Did you run this again?' is the trigger that caught it.
fix: CLAUDE.md updated with continuation-session hydration rule
lesson: Summaries are stale the moment they're written. Hydration reads live state. Never substitute one for the other.
resolution: CONTINUATION SESSION RULE added to CLAUDE.md (explicit, non-negotiable). Rule fires every context-compaction continuation. Verified present 2026-04-21.

description: RESOLVED:  detection-without-remediation — systems that can identify problems but cannot fix them. A subclass of rendered-output-gap (data exists but user can't see it).
No newsletter produced for 2026-04-13 — past 06:00 MT deadline
prevention: Nel audit + Sam test coverage deployed

description: Constitutional SELF-EVOLUTION CYCLE step 10 (AGENT REFLECTION) was spec-only in AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md. The 5 runner bash files wrote evolution entries with pure metrics and no reflection answers. Spec was prose; implementation didn't match. ROOT CAUSE: created the spec, declared it done in reflection (question 3: 'does it have a life?'), gave surface-level answer ('it's in the constitution') without checking the execution layer. FIX: all 5 runners updated with reflection blocks. PREVENTION: Trigger Validation Gate must always chase to the execution layer — 'is it mentioned?' is not 'does code run it?'
fix: all 5 runners updated with v2.0 evolution entries including reflection
lesson: The spec is not the implementation. Always verify the code matches the constitution. The question 'does it have a life' means 'does code actually execute it', not 'is it written in a document'.
resolution: Step 10 (AGENT REFLECTION) implemented in AGENT_ALGORITHMS.md line 281 with mandatory reflection questions. agent-growth.sh sources and runs run_growth_phase which includes reflection block. Wired into all runners.

description: Operating model changed (constitutional v3.0: autonomy, guidance, evolution lifecycle, question framework, dead-loop detection) but CLAUDE.md was not updated. CLAUDE.md bootstraps every session — a fresh Kai would have read stale operating rules. Hyo had to prompt: 'Did you update the new agent protocol?' ROOT CAUSE: end-of-session checklist only said 'update KAI_BRIEF and KAI_TASKS' — did not include CLAUDE.md or any propagation check. No reflection question asked 'did all governing docs get updated?' FIX: (1) Added propagation check as question 6 in Kai POST-TASK REFLECTION, (2) Added as question f in agent AGENT REFLECTION, (3) Added step 4 to CLAUDE.md end-of-session checklist. PREVENTION: Every reflection now asks 'did this change reach every document that governs behavior?' — constitutionally, not as a memory item.
fix: CLAUDE.md updated + propagation check added to both reflection loops + end-of-session checklist
lesson: The session bootstrap file (CLAUDE.md) is the most important governance doc. If the operating model changes and CLAUDE.md doesn't, the next session starts wrong. This is the same class as spec-without-implementation: the change existed in one place but not all the places that matter.
resolution: CLAUDE.md verified 2026-04-21: contains autonomy model, guidance protocol, evolution lifecycle, closed-loop, agent growth rules, ARIC cycle, error-to-gate protocol. Constitutional v3.0 fully propagated.

description: Kai skipped GPT cross-check when rewriting analysis files. Reformatted executive summary without re-sending to GPT-4o for verification. Treated existing GPT reviews as sufficient when the analysis content had changed. This defeats the dual-model pipeline.
prevention: GPT_VERIFIED marker added to every analysis file (YES/NO with timestamp). Pre-commit gate script (kai/protocols/verify-gpt-gate.sh) blocks committing any Analysis file where GPT_VERIFIED != YES. Scheduled task prompt updated with explicit GPT gate step. ANALYSIS_BRIEFING.txt updated with mandatory GPT verification section. Pattern: never assume prior GPT review covers a rewritten analysis.

description: Kai built single-phase GPT pipeline (send finished analysis for review) instead of Hyo's specified dual-phase pipeline (GPT analyzes raw logs independently FIRST, then reviews Kai's work). GPT was rubber-stamping instead of forming independent conclusions. Hyo caught it: 'Why did you choose to take a shortcut when I gave you specific instructions?' Root cause: Kai substituted own interpretation ('cross-check' = review) for Hyo's explicit specification (independent analysis + comparative review).
prevention: gpt_crosscheck.py rewritten as dual-phase (v2): Phase 1 sends raw logs to GPT for independent analysis, Phase 2 sends Kai's analysis for comparative review. ANALYSIS_BRIEFING.txt Section 14 updated with full history of failures (v0 skip, v1 shortcut, v2 correct). Scheduled task prompt specifies both phases explicitly. Pattern: when Hyo gives specific instructions, implement EXACTLY what was specified — do not substitute your own interpretation of what 'should' work.

description: Tuesday April 14 ending balance disputed: Kai reported $103.67 (snapshot ~19:57 MT), GPT found $105.80 (snapshot ~20:58 MT), raw log shows $107.36 (final at 21:29 MT). All three are intermediate snapshots at different times. True ending balance from bot's last metrics cycle: $107.36. P&L for day: $20.92 (not $17.23). NOTE: bot-reported balance may still differ from actual Kalshi account balance due to phantom position issue.
fix: Need to reconcile against actual Kalshi portfolio endpoint. Analysis file Tuesday P&L numbers need updating. aether-metrics.json currentBalance should be $107.36.
lesson: Always use the LAST log entry for balance, not an intermediate snapshot. Time-of-measurement matters.

description: Root PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md and agents/aether/PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md are separate physical files. Only agents/aether/ version was updated to v2.5; root copy stayed at v2.4. Hyo opened root copy from Finder and saw stale version, creating confusion about whether the protocol had been updated.
prevention: Root copy is now a symlink to agents/aether/ — one physical file, impossible to drift. kai_analysis.py injects protocol at startup. KNOWLEDGE.md and CLAUDE.md document the rule.
gate_question: Is PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md at root a symlink (ls -la)? NO → stop and fix before any protocol update.
resolution: Symlink confirmed: PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md -> agents/aether/PROTOCOL_DAILY_ANALYSIS.md (lrwxr-xr-x). Single physical file. Task #16 completed 2026-04-18.

description: Duplicate const declaration in renderAnt() (hq.html) — two const incomeStreams in same function scope caused SyntaxError crashing entire Ant tab
detection: Hyo reported Ant site not working. Console check: SyntaxError: Identifier 'incomeStreams' has already been declared at line 2987
fix: Removed second declaration. Use already-declared variable from earlier in scope.
prevention_gate: After any hq.html edit: open browser console, navigate to Ant tab, confirm no SyntaxError before declaring done. Gate question: 'Did I add any const/let/var that might already exist in the same function scope?'
resolution: Verified 2026-04-21: only one const incomeStreams in hq.html (line 2769). Duplicate was already removed in prior session.

description: Daily audit (2026-04-22): kai/queue/daily-audit.sh defaults HYO_ROOT to $HOME/Documents/Projects/Hyo — when run by scheduled task without HYO_ROOT set it resolves to /sessions/clever-nice-cerf/Documents/Projects/Hyo (dead path), reports all 5 agents FAIL, and writes the report to an unreachable location. The scheduled-task runner at kai/queue/com.hyo.daily-audit.plist (or equivalent) must export HYO_ROOT=/Users/kai/Documents/Projects/Hyo before invoking, OR the script should fall back to its own location via dirname. Self-sabotaging audit — fix before next 22:00 MT run.
